course_description: This course focuses on the management of clients within the child, adolescent and adult mental health systems and the justice system. The course includes consideration of the speech pathologist’s role in managing complex issues in speech pathology clinical practice.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable

Additional timetable information
A detailed course outline is provided on the SPCH3212 Blackboard site withinﾠthe 'Learning resources' content area.
course_aims:
In this course, students are introduced to the key characteristics of major mental health and neurodevelopmental diagnoses across the lifespan. Students will gain an understanding of the relevant legislation, policies, position statements, practice guidelines, key frameworks and philosophies which guide speech pathology practice in the mental health and justice settings. Through the presentation of authentic case-based scenarios, students will develop skills in formulating person- and family-centred management plans to address social, emotional, behavioural, educational and occupational goals for people with communication and swallowing difficulties in mental health and justice. Through active engagement with online learning resources and peer-to-peer learning activities, students will gain insight into the importance of therapeutic relationships and interprofessional practice when providing speech pathology services in the mental health and justice settings. Guest lecturers with professional or personal experience in mental health and justice are a feature of the course.

This course offers students a flipped approach to learning. Students engage in online learning resources before weekly tutorials where they then participate in authentic learning experiences with peers and teaching staff. In the tutorials, students engage in peer-to-peer learning through active participation in independent, small group and large group inquiry-based learning activities. This provides students with more flexibility to learn, but the associated workload is similar to that of a traditional course.

learning_outcomes.description: Describe and advocate for the role of speech pathology within mental health and justice. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6, 1.7, 2.2, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1)
Explain the importance of therapeutic relationship when providing speech pathology services in the mental health and justice setting. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 2.1, 2.6, 3.1)
Describe the key characteristics of major mental health and neurodevelopmental diagnoses across the lifespan. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.3, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3)

Formulate person- and family-centred management plans to address social, emotional, behavioural, educational and occupational goals for people with communication and swallowing difficulties in the mental health and justice setting. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1-1.7, 2,5, 3.4)
Advocate for interprofessional practice in managing people with communication and swallowing difficulties in the mental health and justice setting. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.4, 1.5, 1.7, 2.5, 2.6)
Reflect on your professional support and supervision needs to work within your scope of practice in the mental health and justice setting. (SPA PS Domains/Standards PS 2.1-2.5)

assessment_summary.title: Managing my mental wellbeing reflection task

assessment_details.title: Managing my mental wellbeing reflection task
assessment_details.task_description: Students will submit a video diary or written reflection on what they currently do to manage their own mental wellbeing, and actions they can take to ensure they are implementing sustainable strategies as they progress with their career. A detailed task description will be uploaded to the SPCH3212 Blackboard site. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: In-semester Exam
assessment_details.task_description: This in-semester exam assesses content taught in weeks 1-6, relating to the role of the speech pathologist in the management of children, adolescents and adults with communication and swallowing disorders within mental health and justice settings. It will also assess content related to mental health conditions and importance of interprofessional practice. The exam will encompass short answer questions, including case-based questions. This exam will be delivered in Inspera Assessment and will be completed on your personal laptop on campus. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Case-based assignment
assessment_details.task_description: In this assignment, students will be introduced to a young person in the justice system with mental health needs. Students will be provided with initial assessment data and asked to develop an easy English assessment report, goal plan, management plan, and session plan. Students will video-record themselves delivering the report and plan to their client. The assignment must be supported by evidence from research literature and show knowledge of the youth justice and mental health context. A detailed task description will be uploaded to the SPCH3212 Blackboard site. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Foundation Lectures Two lectures will be held in weeks 1 and 13. The first lecture will provide students with understanding of important concepts and principles relevant to speech pathology practice in mental health and justice contexts. The final lecture will bring together content taught throughout the semester and discuss the future of speech pathology in mental health and justice. Both lectures will be delivered on campus. It is highly recommended that students view both lectures. Lecture recordings will be made available.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 12 (28 Jul - 26 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Online self-direted learning These self-paced modules will further unpack the role of speech pathologists within child, adolescent, and adult mental health and justice systems. Through case examples and applied learning, students will explore the importance of therapeutic relationships, and describe major mental health and neurodevelopmental diagnoses across the lifespan. The series also emphasises interprofessional collaboration, advocacy, and the importance of professional support and supervision to work safely and effectively within scope of practice in these complex settings. Students are required to complete online learning activities prior to attendance and participation in the weekly tutorials.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 12 (04 Aug - 26 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Weekly on-campus tutorials The tutorials will be conducted from weeks 2-12 of semester and will incorporate inquiry-based learning and small group discussion to further explore and apply principles learned in the online modules. Tutorials have been designed for experiential learning and therefore, students are expected to attend and participate in all tutorials. It is essential that students prepare for weekly tutorials by completing the online learning modules and associated preparation activities. As students will be working in small groups during this learning activity, tutorials will not be recorded.
